top_findings.title: HTTP server not wired to the router — no route reachable at runtime
top_findings.evidence: src/book_api_http.erl:12-19 starts httpd with modules=[book_api_http] but no do/1 callback is exported anywhere; book_api_handler is referenced by nothing but its own module line, so handle_request/1 is dead code.
top_findings.suggestion: Export a do/1 that adapts inets mod_data to book_api_handler:handle_request/1, or use cowboy with a dispatch table.

top_findings.title: ETS keypos bug: GET/PUT/DELETE by id always return not_found
top_findings.evidence: src/book_api_db.erl:17 {keypos,2} but insert {NextId,NewBook} at :69 makes the map the key; ets:lookup(books,Id) at :42,:81,:103 never matches an integer id.
top_findings.suggestion: Use default keypos 1 so the integer NextId at position 1 is the key.

top_findings.title: POST /books create path: binary-vs-atom key mismatch always yields 400
top_findings.evidence: handler parse_json builds binary keys (src/book_api_handler.erl:210-244) but create_book/1 matches atom keys #{title:=_,author:=_} (src/book_api_db.erl:50) -> {error,invalid_data}. The correct book:parse_json/1 (src/book.erl:64) is never used by the handler.
top_findings.suggestion: Route the create path through book:parse_json/1, or match binary keys in create_book.

top_findings.title: Storage is in-memory ETS + ad-hoc file dump, not SQLite/embedded DB
top_findings.evidence: src/book_api_db.erl:17 ets:new(books,[named_table,public,{keypos,2}]); persistence is an io_lib:format term dump (src/book_api_db.erl:116-147). No DETS/Mnesia/SQLite.
top_findings.suggestion: Use DETS or Mnesia (disc_copies), or a SQLite NIF/port driver.

top_findings.title: CRUD 'integration' tests are ?assert(true) stubs — test_coverage=1.0 is misleading
top_findings.evidence: test/book_api_test.erl:117-125 handler_health_endpoint/0 and handler_books_crud/0 are just ?assert(true). No insert->lookup round-trip is tested, so the passing suite is blind to all four confirmed defects.
top_findings.suggestion: Add a create->get/update/delete round-trip test asserting the created id is retrievable.
